statement.en: Methodological Framework for Mapping Insulin Supply Chains, Pricing, and Patient Outcomes in Comoros: A Political Economy Analysis, 2021–2026
statement.summary: This methodology article presents a novel framework for conducting a political economy analysis (PEA) of insulin access in the Comoros. It addresses a critical gap in understanding the systemic barriers to diabetes care in small island developing states. The research problem centres on the urgent need to elucidate the complex interplay between supply chain actors, pricing policies, and patient outcomes that perpetuate insulin insecurity. The proposed multi-method approach, implemented between 2021 and 2026, integrates three components: (1) supply chain mapping via stakeholder interviews and document review to trace insulin flows from import to point-of-care; (2) a policy analysis of national pricing regulations, tariffs, and procurement mechanisms; and (3) a longitudinal assessment of pati

proposer: Mariama Abdallah, Fatima Youssouf, Ahmed Soilihi

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: The axiom describes a methodological framework (2021–2026) presented in 2026, still under implementation/validation. The abstract is incomplete (cuts off mid-sentence at 'pati[ent outcomes]'), suggesting pre-publication status. The framework itself is provisionally structured but empirical results and validation remain in progress or unreported.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 流れゆく薬は、権力の網目に捕らえられ、患者のもとに届く。

visualGlyph.interpretation: 島の孤立は、複数の利害関係者による網の目をつくり、生命線を圧縮する。
